hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (701) ran below the Top 100 average (1,477, a 47% ratio), and overheal (61.5%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (60.4%) by 1.1 points - a solid 59th-percentile kill, the only one this raid where Fuggler ranked 1st of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing. 1 real death logged (Hezrun).
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 45.6 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share (47.3% vs 1.7%) - the largest such gap of the raid's first half, with the top rank (guid 25423) still present at 35.6%.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Earth Shield (4 casts), Mana Tide Totem, Ancestral Swiftness, and Mana Potion were all cast, roughly in line with Top 100 norms - no real cooldown gaps this kill. Hezrun's death has no cooldown nearby worth calling a save - the nearest cast (Earth Shield, aimed elsewhere) landed 15.2 seconds later.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (89.2% vs 77.2% average, +12.0) ran above the Top 100 norm; top-target concentration (39.6% vs 47.5% average) tracked modestly below it, across 11 distinct targets, the narrowest spread of the raid.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (846) ran below the Top 100 average (1,776, a 48% ratio), and overheal (42.9%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (41.6%) by 1.3 points - a solid 63rd-percentile kill, ranking 3rd of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing, with 1 real death logged (Staubaoz).
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 30.2 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share (33.1% vs 2.9%), continuing the same pattern seen on Hydross, with the top rank (guid 25423) still leading overall (54.2%).
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched Top 100 norms. Staubaoz's death has no cooldown nearby worth calling a save - the nearest cast (Earth Shield, aimed elsewhere) landed 8.0 seconds later.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (60.9%) and top-target concentration (22.1%) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 23 distinct targets.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,018) ran below the Top 100 average (1,990, a 51% ratio), on a strong 74th-percentile kill, ranking 2nd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 3rd by raw healing, with 5 real deaths logged.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 12.9 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share - the smallest such gap of the raid's first half - with the top rank (guid 25423) leading comfortably here at a real, benchmark-typical share.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched Top 100 norms. None of the 5 real deaths lines up closely with a cooldown that could plausibly have prevented it - the nearest casts (Mana Potion, Earth Shield, Mana Tide Totem, all 5-45 seconds removed) are coincidental timing.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (66.3%) and top-target concentration (18.3%) both tracked in line with the Top 100 norm, across 22 distinct targets.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (971) ran below the Top 100 average (2,177, a 45% ratio), on a middling 47th-percentile kill, ranking 4th of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 3rd by raw healing, with zero real deaths logged - a clean kill.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: This is the one kill this raid where the benchmark's own top-rank Chain Heal (guid 25423) is the real composition gap, not the off-rank one: it ran 38.3 points behind its own 83.7% Top 100 benchmark share, landing at just 45.4% here - Fuggler's composition also included a real, unusual Healing Wave presence (15.1% combined across two ranks) not seen on most other kills this raid.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Tide Totem went uncast against a real 58% Top 100 usage norm - the only real cooldown gap this kill. No deaths to answer.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (61.4% vs 71.7% average, -10.3) and top-target concentration (33.2% vs 35.3% average) both tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, across 22 distinct targets.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (789) ran below the Top 100 average (1,546, a 51% ratio), and overheal (48.4%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (48.0%) by a narrow 0.4 points - a middling 50th-percentile kill, ranking 2nd of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing, with zero real deaths logged.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 38.4 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share, continuing the same pattern seen on most kills this raid, with the top rank (guid 25423) still leading overall.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched Top 100 norms in count. No deaths to answer.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (55.7% vs 53.4% average) and top-target concentration (27.0% vs 23.9% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 22 distinct targets.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (695) ran below the Top 100 average (1,091, a 64% ratio - the closest ratio to benchmark of the raid), and overheal (44.7%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (41.2%) by 3.5 points - a strong 72nd-percentile kill, ranking 1st of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 2nd by raw healing, with 4 real deaths on the raid's longest fight.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 50.5 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share - the largest such gap of the raid's first half - continuing the same pattern seen all night, with the top rank (guid 25423) running well under its own benchmark share here.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Dark Rune went uncast against a real 51% Top 100 usage norm - the one real cooldown gap this kill (matching the same gap seen in Fuggler's other report on this raid tier). None of the 4 real deaths lines up closely with a cooldown that could plausibly have prevented it - the nearest casts (Ancestral Swiftness, Earth Shield, 9-22 seconds removed) are coincidental timing.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (46.1%) and top-target concentration (15.2%) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across the raid's widest spread (27 distinct targets).

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: The raid's low point: HPS (365) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,132, a 32% ratio - the weakest of the raid), and overheal (62.6%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (50.6%) by 12.0 points - the largest overheal gap of the raid - landing at a weak 20th-percentile result, the raid's lowest. Fuggler ranked 3rd of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing, with 1 real death logged.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: This is the raid's most unusual composition: a third distinct Chain Heal rank (guid 1064) led at 41.8%, the off-rank cast (guid 10623) carried another 26.4%, and Earth Shield itself reached a real 21.3% of total healing - well above its typical share - while the benchmark's own top-rank Chain Heal (guid 25423) fell to just 10.5% here, 45.4 points behind its own 55.9% Top 100 share, the single largest composition gap of the raid.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Tide Totem went uncast against Top 100 norms - one of three kills this raid with that same gap (alongside Karathress and Kael'thas). The one real death has no cooldown nearby worth calling a save.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (68.1%) and top-target concentration (27.9%) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 23 distinct targets.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,406, the raid's highest raw HPS) still ran below the Top 100 average (2,664, a 53% ratio), and overheal (33.5%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (25.0%) by 8.5 points - a weak 39th-percentile kill, ranking 3rd of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing, with zero real deaths logged.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 27.3 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share, continuing the same pattern seen all raid night.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched Top 100 norms. No deaths to answer.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (50.0%) and top-target concentration (17.1%) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 23 distinct targets.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (580) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,670, a 35% ratio), and overheal (68.2%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (56.7%) by 11.5 points - the second-largest overheal gap of the raid - landing at a weak 34th-percentile kill, ranking 2nd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank but 3rd by raw healing. Zero real deaths logged, a clean kill despite the low percentile.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 48.4 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share, one of the largest gaps of the raid, continuing the same pattern seen all night.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Ancestral Swiftness went uncast against a real 71% Top 100 usage norm - the only real cooldown gap this kill. No deaths to answer.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (46.1%) and top-target concentration (12.4%) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 24 distinct targets.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: This was by far the raid's most chaotic kill: 16 real deaths, and active time collapsed to 64.8% (Top 100 average 86.9%, a real 22.1-point gap) on a fight that still ended in a real kill. HPS (512) ran to just 50% of the Top 100 average (1,034), and overheal (50.7%) sits just 0.2 points above the sample's single worst parse - producing a weak 36th-percentile result. Fuggler ranked 3rd of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing.
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran 46.0 points ahead of its Top 100 benchmark share, continuing the same pattern seen on every kill this raid night.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Tide Totem went uncast against Top 100 norms - one of three kills this raid with that same gap. With 16 real deaths in a single collapsed encounter, no individual cooldown timing reads as a specific missed save; this is raid-wide breakdown, not a targeted gap.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (59.0%) and top-target concentration (28.7%) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across the raid's widest spread (26 distinct targets) - consistent with the chaos of 16 real deaths.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: Fully consistent across all 10 kills - no slot varies at all, and all 9 enchantable slots carry a permanent enchant on every kill - the same clean gear result as Fuggler's other report for this raid tier.
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: No missing-enchant gaps this raid night. Consumables again tell a different story: Guardian Elixir was never active on any of the 10 kills, and neither was Flask - only Battle Elixir (Healing Power) and Well Fed food were the consistent pairing, matching the same gap seen in Fuggler's other report.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: All 10 real bosses attempted this raid night were killed. Fuggler ranked 1st of 5 tracked healers present by item-level rank on 2 of 10 kills (Hydross, Vashj; 49% average rank percentile) and 1st by raw healing on 1 of 10 (Hydross, the only kill this raid combining both 1st-place ranks). The single most consistent pattern all raid night: an off-rank Chain Heal cast (guid 10623) ran ahead of its own Top 100 benchmark share on 8 of 10 kills, from +12.9 points (Leotheras) to +50.5 points (Vashj) - the same pattern already confirmed in Fuggler's other report. On the remaining 2 kills (Karathress, Al'ar), the gap flipped direction: the benchmark's own top-rank Chain Heal (guid 25423) fell well behind its own benchmark share instead, and Al'ar specifically showed the raid's most unusual composition, leaning on a third distinct Chain Heal rank (guid 1064) and unusually heavy Earth Shield healing. Overheal exceeded the Top 100 sample's own worst parse on 8 of 10 kills - a real, consistent pattern, though most of the individual gaps were narrow (as small as 0.2-1.3 points on 4 of those 8). Percentiles ranged from 20th (Al'ar, the raid's lowest) to 74th (Leotheras), with Al'ar, Solarian (34th), and Kael'thas (36th) the three lowest kills. Kael'thas was the raid's clear low point outside percentile terms too: 16 real deaths and active time collapsing to 64.8%, though the kill still succeeded. Fuggler had zero self-deaths this raid night, unlike the previous report.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Kael'thas saw 16 real deaths and active time collapse to 64.8% (Top 100 average 86.9%) - by far the raid's most chaotic kill, though it still ended in a real kill. Overheal exceeded the Top 100 sample's own worst parse on 8 of the 10 kills this raid night, and Guardian Elixir was never active on any kill, matching the same consumable gap already confirmed in Fuggler's other report.

GearCheckItems.Description: All 9 enchantable slots carry a permanent enchant on every kill
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: No missing-enchant gaps this raid night, matching the same clean result in Fuggler's other report for this raid tier.

GearCheckItems.Description: No Guardian Elixir active on any kill; no Flask either
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: Only Battle Elixir (Healing Power) and Well Fed food were consistently active across all 10 kills this raid night. Guardian Elixir was never up - the same incomplete consumable pattern already confirmed in Fuggler's other report.
